panels.title: Application Up
panels.description: Prometheus scrape status: 1 = the instance is responding, 0 = unreachable.

panels.title: Uptime
panels.description: Time elapsed since the JVM last restarted.

panels.title: Decision Rate
panels.description: Rate of security decisions (ALLOW + BLOCK), all verdicts combined.

panels.title: Block Rate
panels.description: Share of BLOCK decisions over the last 15 minutes. Green < 5%, orange 5-15%, red >= 15%.

panels.title: Security Bypass (1h)
panels.description: Number of security-control bypass events in the last hour. Should stay at 0.

panels.title: OSV Incremental Sync Freshness (hourly)
panels.description: Seconds since the last successful hourly OSV incremental sync (npm/pypi/maven). A high value signals a stuck or failing scheduler. Covers only sync_type="incremental" -- see "OSV Full Sync Freshness" for the nightly cron.

panels.title: OSV Full Sync Freshness (nightly cron)
panels.description: Seconds since the last successful nightly full sync (OSV npm/pypi/maven zips + GHSA/OpenSSF/GitLab git advisories, 2am cron). Expected to grow to ~24h between runs, unlike the hourly incremental sync above. Covers sync_type="full".

panels.title: MITM CA Certificate Expiry
panels.description: Time remaining before the MITM CA certificate expires (generated with a 10-year validity, see MitmCertificateFactory). Once it expires, TLS interception breaks for every client that trusts it. Also exposed at /actuator/health as the "caCertificate" component (DEGRADED under 30 days, DOWN once expired).

panels.title: GitOps Sync Freshness
panels.description: Seconds since the last successful GitOps sync (enterprise governance rules).

panels.title: Security Overhead (avg / p50 / p95) by Decision
panels.description: Time spent in security checks only (excluding binary relay), by allow/block verdict: average, median and 95th percentile.

targets.expr: sum by (decision) (rate(silicaproxy_controller_security_overhead_seconds_sum{job="silicaproxy", instance=~"$instance"}[5m])) / sum by (decision) (rate(silicaproxy_controller_security_overhead_seconds_count{job="silicaproxy", instance=~"$instance"}[5m]))

panels.title: TLS MITM Handshake (avg / p50 / p95)
panels.description: Duration of the TLS handshake during MITM interception: average, median and 95th percentile.

panels.title: HikariCP Connection Pool
panels.description: PostgreSQL connection pool occupancy. 'pending' > 0 signals pool saturation.

panels.title: JVM Threads (by state)
panels.description: Number of JVM threads by state (runnable, waiting, blocked...). Virtual threads are not counted individually.

panels.title: Decisions by Verdict
panels.description: Rate of ALLOW vs BLOCK decisions over time.

panels.title: Local Decision Evaluation (HIT/MISS)
panels.description: DecisionDao.evaluateDecision() checks 3 local sources in a single SQL query: company policy, the locally-synced public vulnerability data (OSV, kept up to date by OsvIncrementalSyncService, not a live call), and api_cache (a still-valid verdict from a previous external call). A HIT means one of the three matched and the verdict is returned with no network call. A MISS means the package/version is unknown to all three, so SecurityService falls through to a registry metadata lookup and then a live OSV/deps.dev fallback check.

panels.title: Decision Rate by Instance (HAProxy Load-Balancing Check)
panels.description: Compares decision throughput across instances to verify HAProxy load balancing.

panels.title: Block Evolution by Type
panels.description: Rate of BLOCK decisions over time, broken down by source: COMPANY_POLICY, PUBLIC_VULN / PUBLIC_VULN_MALWARE / PUBLIC_VULN_ANOMALY (locally-synced OSV data), API_CACHE, REGISTRY_QUARANTINE / REGISTRY_DEPRECATION / REGISTRY_ERROR, EXTERNAL_VALIDATION, or OSV_LIVE / DEPS_DEV (live fallback).

panels.title: External Security API Calls (OSV live / deps.dev)
panels.description: Outbound calls to OSV live and deps.dev when evaluating packages unknown to the local cache, by result.

panels.title: External Validation Calls (sync/async)
panels.description: Calls to configured external validation scanners, in synchronous (blocking) or asynchronous (fire-and-forget) mode.

panels.title: Async Validation Callbacks (by service)
panels.description: Callbacks received from asynchronous validation services, by service and outcome (triggered / error).

panels.title: Block Reasons (external validation, 1h)
panels.description: Volume of blocks decided by external validation over the last hour, by reason (scanner verdict or fail-closed).

panels.title: Incremental OSV Sync (advisories / records, 15m)
panels.description: Volume of advisories and records processed by the hourly incremental OSV sync, by outcome.

panels.title: GitOps Sync (policies / runs, 15m)
panels.description: Volume of synced policies and GitOps runs, by outcome (success/failure).

panels.title: JVM Heap (used / max)
panels.description: JVM heap memory used vs. maximum allocated.

panels.title: Process CPU
panels.description: JVM process CPU usage compared to the host/container system.

panels.title: HTTP Latency (p50 / p95)
panels.description: Incoming HTTP request latency percentiles (requires histogram buckets enabled in application.yaml).

panels.title: HTTP Error Rate (5xx)
panels.description: Rate of HTTP 5xx responses, by status code. Should stay at 0 -- unlike a BLOCK verdict (expected traffic), a 5xx means the proxy itself is failing requests.

panels.title: GC Pause Duration (avg / max)
panels.description: Average and worst-case JVM garbage collection pause duration. A rising avg or a high max explains latency spikes that "Security Overhead" alone wouldn't show.

panels.title: GC Pause Rate (collections/s)
panels.description: Rate of garbage collection pauses, by GC action/cause. A sudden increase usually precedes the duration panel degrading.

panels.title: Core Decision Path Latency (avg / p50 / p95)
panels.description: Average, median and 95th percentile duration of each step on the synchronous per-request decision path: SecurityService, the DecisionDao SQL query, the registry/OSV clients, the proxy stream relay, and URL parsing. All already instrumented via @Timed, just not graphed until now.

panels.title: Metadata & External Validation Verdicts DAO Latency (avg / p50 / p95)
panels.description: Average, median and 95th percentile duration of package_metadata/api_cache reads-writes and the external validation verdicts table (find/findAllByPackage/save), plus the overall ExternalValidationService.check() call.

panels.title: External Validation Cache DAO Latency -- hot path (avg / p50 / p95)
panels.description: Average, median and 95th percentile duration of the ExternalValidationCacheDao methods called on the per-request path (find/upsert/update).

panels.title: External Validation Cache DAO Latency -- cleanup (avg / p50 / p95)
panels.description: Average, median and 95th percentile duration of the background cleanup methods (expired/token deletion, pending expiry sweep) on the external scanner cache.

panels.title: Audit Log Write Outcome (rate)
panels.description: Rate of audit log write attempts, by outcome. FAILURE means AuditLogDao.save() threw (disk full, constraint violation...) and that decision's audit trail was lost -- should stay at 0.

panels.title: Audit Log Latency (avg / p95)
panels.description: Duration of the async audit log write path (AuditLogService.logAudit, including the AuditLogDao.save SQL insert).
